fit: P1c — advising + 30% program/initiative coordination. JD 'What You Will Need': Bachelor's, 2+ yrs higher-ed student-services OR related advising/counseling/mentoring (K-12/non-profits OK), diverse-population experience, large-group communication/presentations, retention/student-development/proactive-advising knowledge, effective with varied audiences, MS Office. 'Would like': Life Sciences background (Jamie has Psych/Education — adjacent, NOT biology; NOT claimed), program-coordination experience (Jamie HAS — strong), SIS/CRM (DegreeWorks/Banner — Jamie does not, candid in cover). 30% coordination (newsletters, orientation, comms schedules, online-learner engagement, advising materials) maps WELL to her InGenius program-coordination + communications + remote-engagement work. Cover candid about higher-ed-office + life-sciences ramp-up. No fabrication of biology knowledge or formal advising-office tenure.
resume_tailoring: Advising + program-coordination blend. Summary role-noun 'student support & program coordination'. InGenius first, bullets blend advise/support + coordinate programs/orientation/comms-schedules/online-learner engagement — full richness. Skills 'Advising & Coordination' cluster. ODN = 2 collective bullets. NextGen/Vestas/Kronos 4 each. Wesleyan leads education, Relevant Coursework kept. Header 'Portland, OR (Open to Relocation)'. No banned content. Personal Statement draws on TRUE identity material (Taiwan -> US international student; scholarship recipient; cross-cultural work incl. 22 nationalities/120 employees at Vestas; Inclusive Leadership pilot scaled to 23 locations) — all from jamie_voice_corpus.md.
